_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox host Braves tonight at 6:45 as Connelly Early faces best team in baseball
items.snark: Nine games under .500 and staring down a 37-18 juggernaut while Fenway boos get warmed up.

items.headline: Will the Red Sox be sellers at the trade deadline despite hanging around playoff race?
items.snark: Potentially worst Sox team in 61 years but only two games out, like watching someone drown in the kiddie pool.

items.headline: Garrett Crochet throwing second live BP Sunday, mid-90s velocity back after shoulder inflammation
items.snark: Crochet felt pleased with his session, which in 2026 Red Sox terms means he only has two more setbacks before September.

items.headline: Roman Anthony takes dry swings without discomfort, still no timetable for return from finger sprain
items.snark: Top prospect swinging a bat pain-free is breaking news when your offense is this historically putrid.

items.headline: Braves 7, Red Sox 6: Four straight losses at Fenway, stranded tying run three nights running
items.snark: Ingredients always in the kitchen, meal never gets made, and your dinner reservations were two hours ago.

items.headline: Patriots begin OTAs Wednesday: Drake Maye leadership, offensive chemistry under microscope
items.snark: Voluntary spring practices begin, which means half the roster shows up and the media pretends it matters.

items.headline: Kyle Williams caught 12 passes as rookie, needs Year 2 jump after invisible 2025 campaign
items.snark: Third-round pick had 12 catches and Patriots fans pretending that's fine because he runs a 4.4 and has potential.
